MOTION TO SUBSTITUTE COUNSEL AND COMPEL TRANSFER OF FILE MATERIALS (TRANSACTION ID # 70213528) FILED BY DEFENDANT BAUER'S INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION, INC. HEARING SET FOR JUL-17-2023 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RE: DEFENDANT BAUER'S INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION, INC.'S MOTION TO SUBSTITUTE COUNSEL AND COMPEL TRANSFER OF FILE MATERIALS. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S UNOPPOSED "MOTION TO SUBSTITUTE COUNSEL AND COMPEL TRANSFER OF FILE MATERIALS" IS GRANTED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motionand Motion To Compel Responses To Requestsfor Production Of Documents (Setone)
Off Calendar Jan-30-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):

Atty. Shawn C. Moore, Esq. YOUNG, MINNEY & CORR, LLP., (Tele: 916 646-1400 ) for Plaintiff. Attorney is here out of the abundance of caution. No other appearances made in this matter.

PLAINTIFF VACAVILLE CHRISTIAN SCHOOL'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests for Production Of Documents (Set one)

Counsel for the Plaintiff stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge, plaintiff, and counsel authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling: The unopposed motion is granted in part and denied in part. It is granted as to the sanctions requested of $1,660, which shall be payable to Plaintiff by Defendant and its counsel (jointly and severally) within fifteen (15) days of notice of entry of order being filed and served by counsel for Plaintiff. The rest of the motion is denied as moot.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: John-Paul Deol; Clerk: R. Sahatjian / M. Goodman; Not Reported. =(302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-29-2022 Set For Trial On Feb-14-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Aug-23-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BAUER'S INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION, INC.'S Motion To Set Aside Default:

Defendant's "motion to set aside default judgment and entry of default" is granted. California courts have a strong policy of deciding cases on their merits (Oliveros v. County of Los Angeles (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 1389, 1396), and this motion was filed well within CCP 473's six-month window. Defendant adequately explains its inadvertence, excusable neglect and/or mistake. (Id.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
